company.name: Axsome Therapeutics
company.subtitle: Auvelity $507M · AXS-05 AD agitation PDUFA Apr 30, 2026 · $16.5B peak sales potential
company.cashRunway: Cash flow positive trajectory (revenue growing 66% YoY)

us.patientsK.note: NAMI/NIMH 2024: ~8M US MDD on treatment; ~21M any depressive episode; SSRIs/SNRIs first-line.

eu.patientsK.note: EU depression registries: ~12M MDD on treatment EU27+UK.

row.patientsK.note: WHO 2023: ~280M global depression; treatment gap >60% in LMICs.

us.wtpPct.note: ~80% - Medicare Part D broad SSRI/SNRI; Spravato (esketamine) with REMS; Auvelity/Exxua newer branded with PA.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~65% - EU broad SSRI/SNRI; Spravato approved, NICE/G-BA conditional.

row.wtpPct.note: ~25% - Japan broad; LMICs minimal branded access.

us.priceK.note: Blended $1.5K: sertraline/fluoxetine/venlafaxine generics $0.3K (85%), Auvelity $8K, Spravato $32K, Exxua $7K.

eu.priceK.note: ~$0.7K blended; EU generic-heavy, Spravato ~60% of US.

penPct.note: ~60% peak - generics saturate; branded growth in TRD (Spravato, Auvelity) and rapid-onset pipeline (psilocybin, zuranolone).

us.reachPct.note: Auvelity MDD approved Aug-2022; ~21M US MDD pts, ~7M TRD; 1.6% reach (~120K US branded-MDD patients peak) is ~3.4x 2025 sales -- defensible growth, recalibrated from 2.5% which implied 5.3x sales cap.

us.priceK.note: ~$7.5K/yr WAC; ~$4.5K net after PBM rebates (~40% GTN).

us.wtpPct.note: 45% -- Express Scripts + CVS formulary tier 3; step through 2 generics; KOL traction for rapid onset (1wk vs 6wk SSRI).

eu.reachPct.note: 5% -- no EMA filing; AXSM focused on US commercial.

us.patientsK.note: Narcolepsy Network/NINDS: ~200K US narcolepsy (type 1 + type 2); Xywav/Xyrem/Wakix/Sunosi approved.

row.patientsK.note: Global: ~3M narcolepsy; orexin agonist pipeline (TAK-861, ALKS 2680) expanding.

us.wtpPct.note: ~80% - Medicare Part D covers Xywav/Xyrem with REMS; Wakix, Sunosi under PA; orexin agonist pipeline potentially curative for NT1.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~60% - EMA approved oxybates, Wakix; EU pricing lower than US.

row.wtpPct.note: ~20% - Japan covered; LMICs modafinil generic only.

us.priceK.note: Blended $45K: Xywav $190K (30%), Xyrem generic $40K (20%), Wakix $60K (15%), Sunosi $15K (15%), modafinil generic $2K (20%).

eu.priceK.note: ~$25K blended; EU oxybates ~55% of US list.

row.priceK.note: ~$6K; Japan near EU, rest modafinil generic.

penPct.note: ~45% peak - oxybate access strong; orexin agonist pipeline (TAK-861) potentially transformative for NT1 (~30% of narcolepsy).

us.reachPct.note: Sunosi EDS narcolepsy/OSA acquired from Jazz 2022; ~400K US EDS; 3.5% reach = ~14K patients peak (~4x 2025 $130M sales). Recalibrated from 12% which implied 13x sales cap -- implausible for a generic-adjacent DEA-IV with Xywav/Wakix competition.

us.wtpPct.note: 40% -- Schedule IV DEA; PBMs step-edit vs modafinil generics; niche vs Xywav/Wakix.

eu.reachPct.note: 8% -- Jazz/Axsome EU rights; launched 2020 by Jazz; NICE/G-BA reimbursed.

eu.wtpPct.note: 18% -- HTA covered for narcolepsy but budget-capped.

us.patientsK.note: NAMI/NIMH 2024: ~6M US GAD treated; ~19M any anxiety disorder; SSRIs/SNRIs first-line.

eu.patientsK.note: EU mental health: ~8M GAD treated EU27+UK; SSRI/SNRI dominant.

row.patientsK.note: WHO 2023: ~300M global anxiety disorders; treatment gap >70% in LMICs.

us.wtpPct.note: ~75% - Medicare Part D and commercial broad SSRI/SNRI coverage; benzo tapering trends; psych carve-outs variable.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~65% - EU broad SSRI coverage; pregabalin approved GAD in EU (not US).

row.wtpPct.note: ~25% - Japan SSRI broad; LMICs minimal access.

us.priceK.note: Blended $0.8K: sertraline/escitalopram/venlafaxine generics $0.3K (90%), buspirone generic, pipeline NR-IR $5K+.

penPct.note: ~70% peak - generics saturate; branded limited to novel MoA (orexin, psilocybin, rapid-onset) pipeline.

us.reachPct.note: Symbravo (AXS-07) migraine acute approved Jan-2025 (not GAD -- config label); ~40M US migraineurs; 10% peak reach given CGRP gepant competition.

us.priceK.note: ~$1K/pack WAC per-use; ~$600/pack net after rebates; episodic pricing.

us.wtpPct.note: 40% -- PBMs step-edit vs rizatriptan generics + Nurtec; formulary access moderate.

market.notes: Same molecule as Auvelity (dextromethorphan-bupropion). ADVANCE-2 + ACCORD-2 Phase 3 positive. ~5.8M AD patients in US, majority experience agitation. Only other approved: Rexulti (Otsuka). PDUFA Apr 30, 2026. Expanding to 600 sales reps for launch.

us.patientsK.note: Alzheimer's Association 2024: ~6.9M Americans age 65+ with AD; ~2M early-stage eligible for anti-amyloid.

eu.patientsK.note: Alzheimer Europe: ~7.8M prevalent AD EU27+UK; ~1.5M mild-to-moderate amyloid-positive per PET criteria.

row.patientsK.note: WHO 2023: ~39M global AD; ex-US/EU ~25M, Japan PMDA approved lecanemab (~1M Japan AD).

us.wtpPct.note: ~50% - Medicare Part B covers Leqembi/Kisunla post-CMS 2024 decision with CED monitoring; PET/MRI infrastructure limits access.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~35% - EMA approved Leqembi July 2024 (excl APOE4 homozygotes); NICE rejected, G-BA under review.

row.wtpPct.note: ~8% - Japan PMDA covered; China/emerging markets minimal anti-amyloid access, donepezil generics dominate.

us.priceK.note: Blended $28K: Leqembi $26.5K + Kisunla $32K + donepezil/memantine generics ($2K avg, 70% of treated pts).

eu.priceK.note: ~$15K blended; EU anti-amyloid net ~55% of US list, generics widely used.

row.priceK.note: ~$5K blended; Japan near EU pricing, rest generics.

penPct.note: ~12% peak through 2030 - infusion capacity, PET access, and ARIA monitoring cap near-term reach of amyloid-targeting.

us.reachPct.note: AXS-05 Alzheimer's agitation Ph3 ADVANCE-2 readout; 30% peak reach (2026+) given ~50% of AD pts develop agitation + only Rexulti approved.

us.wtpPct.note: 50% -- payors will cover if labeled; competes vs Rexulti (~$10K/yr); supplemental NDA path.

eu.reachPct.note: 8% -- EU launch 2028+; HTA scrutiny on AD agitation pricing.

eu.wtpPct.note: 18% -- HTA bodies will benchmark vs antipsychotics generic.

market.notes: Esreboxetine (selective NRI). NDA submitted Nov 2024. ~10M US patients. Current SOC limited (Lyrica, Cymbalta — modest efficacy). $1.5-3B peak potential per company guidance.

us.patientsK.note: CDC/NIH: ~4M US fibromyalgia; 2-4% US adults; Lyrica/Cymbalta/Savella FDA-approved.

eu.patientsK.note: EU rheum societies: ~5M fibromyalgia EU27+UK; Lyrica approved in some, not all EU markets.

row.patientsK.note: Global: ~80M fibromyalgia; diagnosis controversial, treatment gap large.

us.wtpPct.note: ~60% - Medicare Part D covers pregabalin/duloxetine generics; milnacipran branded; Journavx (suzetrigine) Jan 2025 expanding.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~40% - EMA did not approve Lyrica for fibro (US-only indication); duloxetine off-label.

row.wtpPct.note: ~15% - Japan Lyrica approved; LMICs limited access.

us.priceK.note: Blended $1.5K: pregabalin/duloxetine generics $0.4K (85%), milnacipran $4K, Journavx $15K acute use.

eu.priceK.note: ~$0.6K blended; EU duloxetine off-label, generics dominant.

penPct.note: ~30% peak - generics dominant; Nav1.8 (Journavx) and novel analgesics limited uptake given dx controversy.

us.reachPct.note: AXS-14 (esreboxetine) fibromyalgia Ph3 planned; ~6M US FM; 15% peak reach vs Lyrica/Savella/Cymbalta generics.

us.wtpPct.note: 35% -- PBMs step-edit heavily in FM given generics; differentiation required.

eu.reachPct.note: 4% -- EU FM market small; no clear filing strategy.

indications.name: ADHD + MDD with EDS + BED + SWD

market.notes: Solriamfetol (Sunosi active ingredient) expanding to ADHD ($1-3B), MDD with EDS ($1-3B), BED ($0.3-0.5B), SWD ($0.5-1B). FOCUS (ADHD) + PARADIGM (MDD-EDS) Phase 3 data expected. Non-stimulant ADHD option.

us.patientsK.note: CDC BRFSS/NAMI 2023: ~6M US on ADHD treatment; ~11M prevalent (pediatric + adult).

eu.patientsK.note: EU ADHD registries: ~4M on treatment EU27+UK; lower diagnosis rates than US.

row.patientsK.note: Global: ~35M on ADHD treatment; Japan Vyvanse approved adults 2019.

us.wtpPct.note: ~75% - Medicaid/commercial broad coverage; stimulant shortages 2022-2024 impacted access; Axsome's solriamfetol (AXS-05) pipeline non-stim.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~55% - EMA approved stimulants, atomoxetine; stricter controls, lower pediatric diagnosis.

row.wtpPct.note: ~25% - Japan adult Vyvanse; LMICs limited methylphenidate access.

us.priceK.note: Blended $2.5K: methylphenidate/amphetamine generics $0.8K (70%), Vyvanse generic 2023 $1.5K, Qelbree $5K, Adzenys $7K.

penPct.note: ~55% peak - Vyvanse generic erosion 2023 compressed branded share; non-stim (Qelbree, solriamfetol) growth.

us.reachPct.note: Solriamfetol ADHD expansion; Ph3 FOCUS; 10% peak reach given Adderall/Vyvanse generics + Qelbree branded.

eu.reachPct.note: 4% -- EU ADHD mostly methylphenidate generic; Jazz Sunosi EU not ADHD.

market.notes: Reboxetine for narcolepsy. ENCORE Phase 3 completed. NDA track. Non-stimulant approach. $0.5-1B peak per guidance.

us.patientsK.note: Narcolepsy Network/NINDS: ~200K US narcolepsy (type 1 + type 2); Xywav/Xyrem/Wakix/Sunosi approved.

row.patientsK.note: Global: ~3M narcolepsy; orexin agonist pipeline (TAK-861, ALKS 2680) expanding.

us.wtpPct.note: ~80% - Medicare Part D covers Xywav/Xyrem with REMS; Wakix, Sunosi under PA; orexin agonist pipeline potentially curative for NT1.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~60% - EMA approved oxybates, Wakix; EU pricing lower than US.

row.wtpPct.note: ~20% - Japan covered; LMICs modafinil generic only.

us.priceK.note: Blended $45K: Xywav $190K (30%), Xyrem generic $40K (20%), Wakix $60K (15%), Sunosi $15K (15%), modafinil generic $2K (20%).

eu.priceK.note: ~$25K blended; EU oxybates ~55% of US list.

row.priceK.note: ~$6K; Japan near EU, rest modafinil generic.

penPct.note: ~45% peak - oxybate access strong; orexin agonist pipeline (TAK-861) potentially transformative for NT1 (~30% of narcolepsy).

us.reachPct.note: AXS-12 (reboxetine) narcolepsy cataplexy Ph3 SYMPHONY; ~170K US narcolepsy; 15% peak reach vs Xywav/Wakix.

us.priceK.note: ~$25K/yr WAC premium orphan pricing if approved.

us.wtpPct.note: 35% -- niche cataplexy; payors step-edit vs SSRIs off-label + Wakix.

eu.reachPct.note: 6% -- EU narcolepsy HTA covered; AXSM partnership TBD.

eu.wtpPct.note: 15% -- HTA accepts rare sleep disorder premium.

mega_bear.narrative: AXS-05 AD agitation CRL + pipeline delays + cash crunch before profitability
events: ✗ FDA rejects AXS-05 for AD agitation — safety or efficacy concern
✗ AXS-14 fibromyalgia CRL — insufficient data
✗ Solriamfetol ADHD Phase 3 misses
→ Single-franchise (Auvelity/Sunosi) company at $700M revenue, cash tight

bear.narrative: AXS-05 approved but slow uptake + limited pipeline conversion
events: ✓ AXS-05 AD agitation approved but Rexulti entrenched
~ AXS-14 fibro approved but market crowded
✗ Solriamfetol ADHD borderline data — competitive vs Vyvanse
→ Revenue grows to ~$1.2B but pipeline peak limited

base.narrative: AXS-05 + AXS-14 approved + solriamfetol expands Sunosi franchise — path to $3B+
events: ✓ AXS-05 AD agitation approved — leverages 600 rep sales force
✓ AXS-14 fibromyalgia approved — large patient population
✓ Solriamfetol ADHD Phase 3 positive — non-stimulant option
→ Revenue trajectory: $1B 2026 → $2-3B by 2029

bull.narrative: Multi-franchise CNS leader — 7 products, $5B+ peak revenue
events: ✓ Auvelity + AXS-05 build $2B+ dextromethorphan franchise
✓ Solriamfetol ADHD blockbuster — $3B+ peak across indications
✓ AXS-14 fibro + AXS-12 narcolepsy fill the portfolio
✓ Fastest-growing CNS pure-play, M&A target

psychedelic_bull.narrative: CNS platform dominance — $10B+ peak — AbbVie/Pfizer acquires at $300+
events: ✓ All 7 indications approved — $5-10B peak revenue
✓ Solriamfetol ADHD replaces stimulants — paradigm shift
✓ AXS-05 smoking cessation hit — massive TAM
✓ AbbVie or Pfizer acquires for CNS franchise at $300+

lastData.title: $639M 2025 revenue (+66% YoY) + AXS-05 PDUFA Apr 30
points: AXS-05 AD agitation: Priority Review, PDUFA Apr 30, 2026
600 rep sales force expansion for AD agitation launch
$16.5B peak sales potential across pipeline (company estimate)

nextCatalyst.title: AXS-05 PDUFA — AD agitation approval
thresholds: FDA approval/CRL for Alzheimer's disease agitation
If approved: launch with 600 expanded sales reps

risks: Still loss-making despite $639M revenue — when does it turn?
AXS-05 = same molecule as Auvelity (dextromethorphan-bupropion) — cannibalization?
Rexulti already approved for AD agitation — second-mover
AXS-14 fibromyalgia market historically disappointing for new entrants
$8B market cap at 12x revenue for a loss-making company
CNS commercial execution is expensive — 600 rep force is costly
Pipeline relies heavily on label extensions of existing molecules

catalysts.title: AXS-12 narcolepsy NDA submission completion
catalysts._rationale: FDA pre-NDA meeting supported NDA submission for AXS-12 in narcolepsy, completion expected January 2026

catalysts.title: AXS-05 Alzheimer's disease agitation PDUFA decision
catalysts._rationale: FDA officially set PDUFA date of April 30, 2026 with Priority Review granted
